title: Move Light/Dark Mode Toggle from Global Setting to Per-User Preference
story: As a DataTools Pro user on a team, I want to choose between light and dark mode independently from my teammates, so that I can use my preferred visual mode without affecting others.
description:
Currently the light/dark mode toggle is a global team setting — changing it affects all users in the organization. This needs to become a per-user personal preference.

## Changes
- Light/dark mode preference stored per individual user instead of at the team/organization level
- Two users on the same team can have different mode preferences
- Color/branding customization (accent color, logo, etc.) remains a team-level setting
- The dropdown toggle for mode selection is made available as a personal preference
acceptance_criteria: Light/dark mode setting is stored per individual user
Two users on the same team can have different mode preferences simultaneously
Color/branding customization remains a team-level setting
Changing mode does not affect other team members
ux_changes: Light/dark mode toggle now applies per individual user rather than globally for the team. Each user can independently choose their preferred mode.
middleware_changes: Mode preference now read from and written to the user record rather than the team/organization settings.
data_model_changes: Mode preference (light/dark) field added to the user-level settings instead of the team-level settings.
implementation_notes: Customer request from Fundcanna. Mode preference moved from organization-level to user-level storage. Color and branding (accent color, logo, favicon) remain team-level. This separation allows individual visual comfort while maintaining team brand consistency.
comment_summary: Developer needed additional clarification on the scope. Product owner explained that light/dark is a personal preference while branding (colors, logo) is team-level. Developer implemented and deployed to development for review. Product owner also requested updating navigation to use nav-pills styling.
